title: Open work permit extensions - Canada.ca
content:
H-1B visa holder work permit

## Open work permit extensions
- Who can extend their H-1B open work permit
- How to extend your H-1B open work permit
As an H-1B visa holder or their family member, you were eligible for an open work permit with a validity period of up to 3 years.
If your permit was issued with a validity period of less than 3 years, you may be eligible for an extension.
Deadline to apply for an extension: December 15, 2026 Updated submission deadline

## Who can extend their H-1B open work permit
To be eligible to extend your open work permit as an H-1B visa holder, you must meet all of the
        following requirements:
- You currently hold a valid H-1B open work permit issued under the H-1B public policy.
- Your current H-1B open work permit is valid for less than 3 years from the date it was issued.
- You apply for an extension before December 16, 2026 Updated submission deadline .
- You’re inside Canada when you apply for your extension.
We can’t issue a permit for longer than the validity of your passport . Your open work permit will be
    issued for up to
    the remainder of the maximum 3-year validity period or to expiry date listed on your passport, whichever comes
    first.

### Family members of H-1B visa holders
To be eligible to extend your open work permit as a family member of an H-1B visa holder, you must meet all
        of the following requirements:
- You currently hold a valid H-1B an open work permit issued as a family member of an H-1B visa holder.
- Your current H-1B open work permit is valid for less than 3 years from the date it was issued.
- The H-1B visa holder is eligible to extend their H-1B open work permit.
- You apply for an extension before December 16, 2026 .
- You’re inside Canada when you apply for your extension.
We can’t issue a visa or permit for longer than the validity of your passport . Your permit will be
    issued to whichever of the following dates is the earliest:
- the validity of the primary H-1B visa holder’s work permit or
- the expiry date listed on your passport

## How to extend your H-1B open work permit
Apply online using your IRCC secure
        account .
This is a different account than you used for your initial H-1B open work permit application.
When you apply, you must
- follow the general
            instructions for how to extend an open work permit and
- complete certain fields in your IMM 5710 form exactly as shown below, depending on whether you’re applying as an H-1B visa holder or a family member of an H-1B visa holder
- an H-1B visa holder or
- a family member of an H-1B visa holder

### Follow the application instructions
You must complete the fields below exactly as shown so we can identify your application under this temporary
        policy. If you don’t follow these instructions exactly
- we won’t be able to confirm that you’re applying as an H-1B visa holder a family member of an H-1B visa holder
- a family member of an H-1B visa holder
- your application may be delayed or refused
Follow the steps below to apply for an open work permit extension as an H­1B visa holder.
Step 1: Read the guide to find out how to apply to extend your open work permit.
Step 2: Complete the IMM 5710 (opens in a new tab) form. Be sure to complete the following field exactly as shown.
- In the “Job Title” field enter “H1BP”
Step 3: Create an account or sign in to the IRCC secure account.
Start your application

### Family members of H-1B visa holders
Follow the steps below to apply for an open work permit extension as a family member of an H-1B visa holder.
Step 1: Read the guide to find out how to apply to extend your open work permit.
Step 2: Complete the IMM 5710 (opens in a new tab) form. Be sure to complete the following field exactly as shown.
- In the “Job Title” field enter “H1BF”
- In the “Brief description of duties” field enter the work permit extension application number of the H-1B visa holder
- enter the work permit extension application number of the H-1B visa holder
Step 3: Create an account or sign in to the IRCC secure account.
Start your application
